title: Pecluma atra
intro: Pecluma atra, also known as Black Lip Fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities that have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pecluma atra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Pecluma atra is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture to leverage its health benefits, and it is commonly used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Pecluma atra is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the polypody fern family. This family comprises over 2000 species of ferns and is one of the largest among all fern families. Pecluma atra is a member of the genus Pecluma, which is a group of epiphytic ferns. The genus Pecluma is further divided into several species, each with unique characteristics. The classification of Pecluma atra reflects its distinct growth habits and physical features.
